title: festuca laevigata subsp. crassifolia
intro: Festuca laevigata subsp. crassifolia, also known as the rough fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the Poaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory problems. Traditionally, the rough fescue is prepared as a tea or infusion, with the dried leaves and stems being steeped in hot water to make a medicinal herbal remedy.
